title: Bulk report - Week 43
summary:
Capesize
The Capesize market was in dire straits this week as tonnage levels were heard to be building while many areas had minimal activity as the 5TC dropped -15,398 to $36,065 week on week. The Pacific Basin was under pressure earlier on in the week but did find a floor mid-week, albeit at a significant discount to the Atlantic Basin. The Transpacific C10 ended the week on an upbeat at $32,979, while the Transatlantic C8 struggled the whole week to find a footing coming off -4300 on Friday alone to settle at $41,600.

children.title: Capesize
children.summary: The Capesize market was in dire straits this week as tonnage levels were heard to be building while many areas had minimal activity as the 5TC dropped -15,398 to $36,065 week on week. The Pacific Basin was under pressure earlier on in the week but did find a floor mid-week, albeit at a significant discount to the Atlantic Basin.

children.title: Panamax
children.summary: The week has seen a large drop in rates across all of the routes, which was not helped by a lack of activity in the first half. With new lows, however, there has been an increase in cargo quoted - enough to sustain the present levels at least for now.

children.title: Ultramax/Supramax
children.summary: A change of direction for the sector as limited fresh enquiry appeared in the market across many areas leading to increased tonnage availability. Period activity was limited but a 63,000-dwt open China was linked to eight to 10 months trading on an index linked rate basis 114% of BSI 10TC.

children.title: Handysize
children.summary: This week the BHSI made some negative moves with both Atlantic and Pacific basins softening. The softening in Asia was suggested to be due to a lack of fresh enquiry.
